senses.definition: a person who opposes intrusion or interference; someone who advocates for minimal or no intrusion
examples: The debate featured several nonintrusionists who argued against government data collection.
As a nonintrusionist, her approach respects personal boundaries in research.

senses.definition: not causing disruption or attention; not intrusive
examples: The survey was designed to be nonintrusive, avoiding personal questions.
They used nonintrusive sensors to monitor the environment.

senses.definition: not easy to understand or figure out; contrary to what seems natural
examples: The controls are nonintuitive, requiring a manual to operate.
The layout is nonintuitive at first glance.

senses.definition: in a way that is not easy to understand or immediately obvious
examples: The results were nonintuitively surprising, challenging our assumptions.
Nonintuitively, increasing complexity reduced efficiency in this case.

senses.definition: the quality of being not intuitive; not easily understood
examples: The nonintuitiveness of the design slowed user adoption.
Users struggled with the nonintuitiveness of the software.

senses.definition: not involving or requiring surgical entry into the body; harmless or minimally invasive
examples: The clinic uses noninvasive imaging techniques.
Noninvasive monitoring reduces patient risk.

senses.definition: in a way that does not involve entering the body or causing damage
examples: The device can monitor heart activity noninvasively.
Noninvasively collected data can still be highly informative.

senses.definition: not turned upside down or upside down relative to a reference point; not inverted
examples: The photograph remained noninverted on the stand.
Keep the display noninverted for clarity.

senses.definition: not causing inversion; relating to or producing a positive or direct effect
examples: The amplifier uses a noninverting configuration.
Noninverting feedback can stabilize the circuit.

senses.definition: not likely to arouse envy or ill will; not biased by jealousy
examples: She offered noninvidious criticism aimed at improving the project.
The award was given in a noninvidious spirit.

senses.definition: in a noninvidious manner; without envy or malice
examples: She criticized the plan noninvidiously, focusing on improvements.
The remark was made noninvidiously to help the team.

senses.definition: the quality of being noninvidious; lack of envy or spite in actions or remarks
examples: The report praised the team’s noninvidiousness in reviews.
Noninvidiousness characterizes his critique.

senses.definition: the state of not being unable to be defeated or overcome; lack of invincibility
examples: The hero’s noninvincibility was challenged by a clever adversary.
Acknowledging noninvincibility can lead to wiser strategies.

senses.definition: not truly invincible; capable of being defeated
examples: Even the champion is noninvincible in certain conditions.
Acknowledging that enemies can be noninvincible helps plan defenses.

senses.definition: the quality of not being invincible; vulnerability
examples: Her noninvincibleness surprised the crowd as she admitted limits.
Noninvincibleness can drive careful preparation.

senses.definition: in a way that is not invincible; showing vulnerability
examples: The team played noninvincibly, aware of their weaknesses.
They approached the match noninvincibly but with determination.

senses.definition: not taking part; not connected or involved
examples: She stayed noninvolved in the debate to avoid conflict.
Keep me noninvolved in the project decisions.

senses.definition: the state of not taking part or not being connected
examples: Her noninvolvement in the project surprised the team.
Noninvolvement can be a deliberate boundary choice.

senses.definition: plural of noninvolvement; instances of not participating or not being connected
examples: Several noninvolvements were noted in the case study.
The report lists multiple noninvolvements across departments.

senses.definition: not treated with or containing iodide; lacking iodine treatment
examples: The product is noniodized and suitable for certain medical uses.
Noniodized salt is common in kitchens.

senses.definition: an atom or molecule that has not gained or lost an electrical charge (a neutral ion)
examples: In this context, a nonion is simply a neutral species in solution.
Chemists study how nonions interact with other particles.

senses.definition: not containing or involving ions with an electric charge; nonionic compounds
examples: Nonionic detergents are gentler on skin.
The solution contains nonionic surfactants.

senses.definition: not ionized; lacking electrical charge due to loss or gain of electrons
examples: The gas remains nonionized under normal conditions.
Nonionized compounds behave differently in solution.

senses.definition: not capable of ionizing atoms or molecules; not producing ions
examples: Nonionizing radiation has less energy per photon than ionizing radiation.
The safety guidelines focus on nonionizing effects.

senses.definition: not having rage or fury; unaroused by anger
examples: He remained calm and nonirate during the dispute.
The judge’s nonirate demeanor helped de-escalate tensions.
